mission.title: falco: [Tracking] Opening multiple event sources in the same Falco instance
mission.description: Support running multiple event sources (syscall, k8s_audit, plugins) in parallel within a single Falco instance. Previously Falco could only open one event source at a time, requiring multiple Falco instances for different sources. Fixed in Falco 0.33.0 via a multi-threaded architecture redesign (PR #2182).

steps.title: Verify Falco version supports multiple sources
steps.description:
Multi-source support was added in Falco 0.33.0. Check your version:

Upgrade to Falco 0.33.0+ if running an older version.

steps.title: Configure multiple event sources
steps.description:
Enable multiple sources in your Falco configuration (falco.yaml):

This configures Falco to use the modern BPF engine for syscalls while simultaneously processing K8s audit events via the plugin.

steps.title: Verify multiple sources are active
steps.description:
Check Falco logs to confirm multiple sources are running in parallel:

You should see log entries for each active source (e.g., "syscall" and "k8s_audit") running concurrently.

resolution.summary: Falco 0.33.0+ supports running multiple event sources in parallel within a single instance using a multi-threaded architecture. Each source runs in its own thread with independent rule processing. This was implemented in PR #2182, which redesigned the event processing engine to support concurrent event sources.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
